summary: Iter8 re-validation of Tours. CRITICAL BLOCKER discovered first: TourButton.js had `import Joyride, { STATUS } from 'react-joyride'` but react-joyride 3.1.0 exposes Joyride only as a NAMED export — frontend would not compile at all (webpack error visible as red 'Compiled with problems' overlay). Applied 1-line testing-agent fix: changed to `import { Joyride, STATUS }`. After that fix, ALL 10 tours walk end-to-end to '¡Listo!' (10/10, BEST result yet — iter6 was 8/10, iter7 was 0/10). The Messages/Scope target='body' fix is now CONFIRMED working: messages reaches step 4 ¡Listo! and scope reaches step 3 ¡Listo!. The iter7 regression (Siguiente closing tour prematurely) is FULLY RESOLVED. Two remaining non-blocking issues: (a) beacon still appears on step 0 of every tour despite root-level disableBeacon — spec said this is non-critical; (b) 'Saltar tour' text button still NOT rendered (only the ✕ Cerrar icon), the display:'inline-block' added to buttonSkip in tourStyles did not surface the skip button.

ui_bugs.issue: BLOCKER (now fixed by testing agent). `import Joyride, { STATUS } from 'react-joyride'` fails because react-joyride 3.1.0's package only exposes Joyride as a NAMED export (per dist/index.d.mts). Webpack overlay 'Compiled with problems' was visible on every page, app effectively unusable until import was fixed to `import { Joyride, STATUS } from 'react-joyride'`. Testing agent applied the fix to unblock validation.

Still NOT rendered as visible button. Portal buttons across all 10 tours are exactly: step0=['Siguiente','Cerrar'], stepN-1=['Atrás','¡Listo!','Cerrar'], middle=['Atrás','Siguiente','Cerrar']. 'Saltar tour' / [data-action='skip'] button is absent. The display:'inline-block' added to tourStyles.buttonSkip did not surface the button. Likely react-joyride v3.1.0 needs a different prop pattern (e.g., explicit visibility on buttonSkip, or hideCloseButton:false + locale.skip, or fontSize+lineHeight to avoid zero-height collapse).

Root-level <Joyride disableBeacon /> prop is NOT honored by react-joyride 3.1.0 — beacon (.react-joyride__beacon) is visible on the first step of every tour and user must click it once to reveal the tooltip. Per main agent spec this is acceptable ('beacon puede o no aparecer, no es crítico'). Documented for awareness.

messages.note: target='body' fix on step 4 CONFIRMED working — reaches ¡Listo!

scope.note: target='body' fix on step 3 CONFIRMED working — reaches ¡Listo!

action_items: Validate / commit the import fix made by testing agent in /app/frontend/src/components/TourButton.js line 9: `import { Joyride, STATUS } from 'react-joyride'` (named export — Joyride 3.x dropped default).

(Optional) Surface the 'Saltar tour' button — current tourStyles.buttonSkip { display:'inline-block' } is insufficient. Try: buttonSkip:{ display:'inline-flex', padding:'8px 12px', visibility:'visible', position:'static', color:'#64748b', fontSize:'12px', fontWeight:600 } + verify the joyride Tooltip component actually requests the skip button — in react-joyride v3 the skip slot may need locale.skip non-empty AND showSkipButton at root (already set) AND the tooltip footer not hidden. Alternatively keep only the ✕ Cerrar button as the skip affordance and remove the requirement.

(Optional / nice-to-have) The first-step beacon is still visible; if main agent wants to suppress entirely, programmatically start with run:true + advance through ORIGIN.BEACON in handleCallback by ignoring it, or upgrade react-joyride to a version where root disableBeacon is honored. Not blocking per current spec.
critical_code_review_comments: TourButton.js: import statement must use named export for react-joyride 3.x. This was the root cause of the iter8 regression — a one-line bug that prevents the entire SPA from compiling.
lib/tours.js Messages step 4 and Scope step 3 changes (target:'body', placement:'center') are correct and validated end-to-end.
Recommendation: add a CI smoke test that simply boots the frontend dev server and asserts the webpack overlay isn't present. This would have caught the iter8 import bug instantly.
updated_files: /app/frontend/src/components/TourButton.js (line 9: changed default import to named import — testing-agent allowed standard import fix)

success_rate.frontend: 100% tours reach ¡Listo! (10/10) AFTER applying import fix. Skip-button rendering remains a medium-priority gap. Beacon visibility on step 0 documented as low priority per spec.

test_credentials: Super Admin: pzsuave007@gmail.com / Uni2mkt007! — login OK
context_for_next_testing_agent: react-joyride 3.1.0 named export: `import { Joyride, STATUS, ACTIONS, EVENTS, LIFECYCLE, ORIGIN, PORTAL_ELEMENT_ID, defaultLocale, defaultOptions, useJoyride } from 'react-joyride'`. NO default export. Always verify with `grep -E '^export' /app/frontend/node_modules/react-joyride/dist/index.d.mts`. To walk a tour in Playwright: click `[data-testid="tour-btn-{key}"]`, then click `.react-joyride__beacon` if visible (still appears in v3.1.0 despite root disableBeacon), then loop reading `#react-joyride-portal` button texts ('Siguiente'/'Atrás'/'¡Listo!'/'Cerrar') and clicking forward. All 10 tour keys validated: dashboard, clients, quotes, invoices, agreements, jobs, calendar, card, messages, scope. Pages: /, /clientes, /quotes, /invoices, /contratos, /trabajos, /calendario, /tarjeta, /mensajes, /scope.
rca of the issue: Iter8 introduced a CRITICAL import regression: TourButton.js was authored with `import Joyride, { STATUS } from 'react-joyride'` which assumes Joyride is a default export. react-joyride 3.x (3.1.0 installed) made Joyride a NAMED-only export — per its dist/index.d.mts the exports are { ACTIONS, EVENTS, Joyride, LIFECYCLE, ORIGIN, PORTAL_ELEMENT_ID, STATUS, defaultLocale, defaultOptions, useJoyride } with no default. Webpack threw `export 'default' (imported as 'Joyride') was not found in 'react-joyride'` and refused to compile any page (red error overlay). Mitigation applied by testing agent: changed line 9 to `import { Joyride, STATUS } from 'react-joyride'`. After this fix the iter7 regression (beacon-click hack desync) was confirmed already reverted and all 10 tours walk end-to-end with the Messages/Scope target='body' fix verified.
